claim_boundary: The ONLY dpar with a documented, in-code-flagged interval-calibration failure: the package's own confint() warns and redirects to profile/bootstrap, and the formal ADEMP pilot quantifies severe Wald undercoverage (up to 40.5% false-positive rate) near nu=0. Point estimates still recover (deterministic tests pass) so this is not "not_implemented," but the interval evidence is a documented problem, not a feasibility demonstration -- hence diagnostic_only rather than interval_feasible. Profile/bootstrap nu intervals do not warn (test-confint-skew-normal-slant.R:35-41) but have no coverage campaign either: the 2026-06-17 pilot README explicitly excludes "profile/bootstrap interval calibration" from its scope, so profile/bootstrap should be read as unvalidated-but-recommended, not as a calibrated fix. REML unavailable regardless (see REML row below).
